name: skill-integration-hooks
goal: Any installed skill automatically benefits from Areté's intelligence layer. Outputs are indexed, surfaced in relevant workflows, and organized in the workspace — without the skill author needing to know about Areté.

userStories.title: Validate integration profile schema against native skills
userStories.description: Write the integration profile YAML for all 9 native skills that have prose integration instructions. Use ONLY the proposed schema (SkillIntegration with outputs array + contextUpdates). For each skill, catalog the current prose instructions (exact text and location in SKILL.md). If any skill's behavior can't be expressed in the schema, document it as a gap before coding. Save as dev/work/plans/skill-integration-hooks/native-skill-profiles.md. Skills to catalog: competitive-analysis, discovery, create-prd, construct-roadmap, general-project, capture-conversation, save-meeting, process-meetings, rapid-context-dump. Mitigates: R2 (migration drift), R5 (scope creep).
acceptanceCriteria: Document exists at dev/work/plans/skill-integration-hooks/native-skill-profiles.md
All 9 native skills have integration profiles written using the proposed schema
Each profile includes the current prose instructions it replaces (exact text + line location)
Any schema gaps are flagged with notes on what can't be expressed
No schema expansion — only the proposed SkillIntegration type is used

userStories.title: Define the Skill Integration Profile schema
userStories.description: Add integration types to packages/core/src/models/skills.ts. Create SkillIntegrationOutput type with fields: type ('project' | 'resource' | 'context' | 'none'), path? (string, pattern with {name} placeholder), template? (string, variant name), index? (boolean). Create SkillIntegration type with fields: outputs? (SkillIntegrationOutput[]), contextUpdates? (string[]). Add integration?: SkillIntegration to SkillDefinition (optional for backward compat). Add integration?: SkillIntegration to SkillMetadata (so frontmatter parsing captures it). SkillCandidate does NOT need integration — routing uses triggers/description, not output config. Update getInfo() in packages/core/src/services/skills.ts to read integration from: (1) SKILL.md frontmatter, (2) .arete-meta.yaml sidecar (merged with frontmatter). Before starting, read: packages/core/src/models/skills.ts (existing types), packages/core/src/services/skills.ts (getInfo, readSkillFrontmatter, readAreteMeta), packages/core/src/services/LEARNINGS.md, packages/core/test/services/skills.test.ts (test patterns). Mitigates R4 (backward compat).
acceptanceCriteria: SkillIntegrationOutput and SkillIntegration types added to packages/core/src/models/skills.ts and exported from index
SkillDefinition has optional integration field
SkillMetadata has optional integration field
SkillCandidate is NOT modified
getInfo() reads integration from SKILL.md frontmatter (native skills)
getInfo() reads integration from .arete-meta.yaml sidecar (community skills)
When both frontmatter and sidecar have integration, sidecar values merge/override
Existing .arete-meta.yaml files without integration field parse correctly (integration is undefined)
Tests: old YAML → integration undefined; new YAML → populated; partial → populated with present fields
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Build integration section generation (pure functions)
userStories.description: Create packages/core/src/utils/integration.ts with standalone functions (NOT on SkillService). (1) generateIntegrationSection(skillId: string, integration: SkillIntegration): string | null — returns null when all outputs are type:none or outputs is empty/undefined; generates markdown section with output location, indexing, and template instructions; uses workspace-relative paths or CLI commands (never skill-relative paths per LEARNINGS.md); for templates uses 'arete template resolve --skill {id} --variant {name}'. (2) injectIntegrationSection(skillMdContent: string, section: string | null): string — uses sentinel markers <!-- ARETE_INTEGRATION_START --> and <!-- ARETE_INTEGRATION_END -->; idempotent (replaces existing if markers found, appends if not); when section is null, removes existing section if markers found, no-ops otherwise. (3) deriveIntegrationFromLegacy(def: SkillDefinition): SkillIntegration | undefined — maps createsProject + projectTemplate → integration profile for native skills without explicit integration fields. Export from packages/core/src/utils/index.ts. Before starting, read: packages/core/src/utils/templates.ts (template resolution pattern), packages/core/src/utils/index.ts (barrel exports), packages/runtime/skills/LEARNINGS.md (path conventions). Mitigates R1 (sentinel markers), R7 (workspace-relative paths).
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/src/utils/integration.ts created with three exported functions
generateIntegrationSection returns correct markdown for project, resource, context output types
generateIntegrationSection returns null for type:none or empty/undefined outputs
Generated section uses workspace-relative paths and CLI commands (not skill-relative)
Template instructions use 'arete template resolve --skill {id} --variant {name}'
injectIntegrationSection uses <!-- ARETE_INTEGRATION_START --> and <!-- ARETE_INTEGRATION_END --> markers
injectIntegrationSection is idempotent (inject twice → same result)
injectIntegrationSection with null section removes existing markers/section
deriveIntegrationFromLegacy maps createsProject:true + projectTemplate:'analysis' → correct SkillIntegration
deriveIntegrationFromLegacy returns undefined when no legacy fields present
All functions exported from packages/core/src/utils/index.ts
Full test coverage in packages/core/test/utils/integration.test.ts
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Wire injection into install and update paths + fix root-level file deployment
userStories.description: Three changes in this task: (A) In SkillService.install() (packages/core/src/services/skills.ts): after writing .arete-meta.yaml, read integration profile via getInfo(), call generateIntegrationSection() + injectIntegrationSection(), write updated SKILL.md. (B) In WorkspaceService.update() (packages/core/src/services/workspace.ts): after syncCoreSkills(), iterate all skills in .agents/skills/ — for each skill call getInfo() → deriveIntegrationFromLegacy() or read explicit integration → generateIntegrationSection() → injectIntegrationSection() → write. Note: .agents/skills/ is IDE-agnostic, no adapter handling needed. (C) Fix root-level file deployment: update create() and syncCoreSkills() to also copy .md files at the skills directory root (not just subdirectories) — this fixes PATTERNS.md deployment (pre-existing bug) and enables the integration guide later. Before starting, read: packages/core/src/services/skills.ts (install method), packages/core/src/services/workspace.ts (update, syncCoreSkills, create — especially listSubdirectories usage), packages/core/src/services/LEARNINGS.md (service patterns), packages/core/src/utils/integration.ts (functions from task-2a). Mitigates R6 (update path wired from day one).
acceptanceCriteria: After arete skill install, SKILL.md has ## Areté Integration section with sentinel markers
After arete update, all skills with integration profiles have sections (regenerated from frontmatter or sidecar)
Re-running inject on same file is idempotent (no section duplication)
Root-level .md files (PATTERNS.md, README.md) in skills source dir are copied to .agents/skills/ during create() and syncCoreSkills()
Community skills are preserved during update (existing isCommunitySkill check still works)
Skill install → verify markers → simulate SKILL.md overwrite → update → verify section restored
Tests for install injection path in skills.test.ts
Tests for update injection path and root-level file copy in workspace tests
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Template resolution for community skills
userStories.description: Confirm resolveTemplatePath() in packages/core/src/utils/templates.ts works by filesystem probe without needing TEMPLATE_REGISTRY entry. The function already checks workspace override → skill-local → legacy paths via filesystem access(). If it works without registry: add a test confirming this. If it doesn't: make resolution fall back to filesystem scan when skill ID not in registry. Add info message when workspace override supersedes skill template in 'arete template resolve' output (packages/cli/src/commands/template.ts). TEMPLATE_REGISTRY is NOT modified for community skills. Before starting, read: packages/core/src/utils/templates.ts (resolveTemplatePath, TEMPLATE_REGISTRY), packages/cli/src/commands/template.ts (resolve command), packages/core/test/utils/templates.test.ts (existing tests). Mitigates R3.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveTemplatePath works for skill IDs not in TEMPLATE_REGISTRY (confirmed via test)
Test: community skill with templates/report.md resolves correctly
Test: workspace override at templates/outputs/{id}/report.md wins over skill template
arete template resolve shows info message when workspace override supersedes skill template
TEMPLATE_REGISTRY is NOT modified for community skills
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Install-time guidance and validation gate
userStories.description: Update the CLI install flow in packages/cli/src/commands/skill.ts. After successful install, print: summary of what integration was configured (output type, path, indexing if available), and guidance message 'Edit .agents/skills/<name>/.arete-meta.yaml to customize, or ask an agent to help'. buildAreteMeta() stays simple — no commented YAML. The validation gate is a manual step: before proceeding to Phase 3, test with a real community skill to verify agent compliance with the appended ## Areté Integration section. Before starting, read: packages/cli/src/commands/skill.ts (installSkillAction, existing post-install messages), packages/cli/test/commands/skill.test.ts (test patterns). Mitigates R9.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful install, CLI prints integration summary (output type, path if configured)
After successful install, CLI prints guidance about editing .arete-meta.yaml or asking an agent
buildAreteMeta() does NOT include commented YAML
Existing install flow (overlap detection, --json, --yes) still works correctly
Tests for new install output messaging
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: Verify existing skill README deployment
userStories.description: The existing packages/runtime/skills/README.md was not deployed to user workspaces because create() and syncCoreSkills() only copy subdirectories. After the root-level file copy fix in task-2b, verify README.md reaches .agents/skills/README.md after install and update. This is primarily a verification task — the fix is in task-2b. Add a test if not already covered. Before starting, read: packages/runtime/skills/README.md, test results from task-2b.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md exists in .agents/skills/ after arete install (create path)
README.md exists in .agents/skills/ after arete update (sync path)
Test coverage confirms root-level .md deployment works for README.md
userStories.notes: Already covered by task-2b tests (4 root-level file deployment tests including README.md)

userStories.title: Create agent integration setup guide
userStories.description: Create packages/runtime/skills/_integration-guide.md. This will be deployed to .agents/skills/_integration-guide.md in user workspaces via the root-level file copy from task-2b. Content should cover: what integration hooks are (behavioral context injection via ## Areté Integration section), the four output types (project, resource, context, none) with examples, how to edit .arete-meta.yaml integration section, example profiles for different skill types, how template resolution works with community skills, when and why to run arete index. The guide should be clear enough that an agent reading it can produce a valid integration profile for any community skill.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/runtime/skills/_integration-guide.md created
Guide covers: output types with examples, .arete-meta.yaml editing, template resolution, indexing
Example profiles for each output type (project-based, resource-based, context-based, conversational/none)
Guide is deployed to .agents/skills/_integration-guide.md via root-level file copy
An agent reading the guide can produce a valid SkillIntegration YAML block

userStories.title: Migrate native skills to use integration profiles
userStories.description: For each of the 9 native skills with prose integration instructions: (1) Add integration fields to SKILL.md frontmatter using the schema from task-1. (2) Remove duplicated prose ('Run arete index', 'Save to resources/', etc.). (3) Verify generateIntegrationSection() produces equivalent instructions to the removed prose. (4) Compare before/after against the catalog from task-0 (native-skill-profiles.md). Skills to migrate: competitive-analysis, discovery, create-prd, construct-roadmap, general-project, capture-conversation, save-meeting, process-meetings, rapid-context-dump. Migrate one at a time, run npm test between each. PREREQUISITE: Phase 2 validation gate passed (agent compliance confirmed). Before starting, read: dev/work/plans/skill-integration-hooks/native-skill-profiles.md (the catalog from task-0), each skill's SKILL.md. Mitigates R2.
acceptanceCriteria: All 9 native skills have integration fields in SKILL.md frontmatter
No prose integration instructions remain in any of the 9 SKILL.md files
Generated ## Areté Integration section for each skill matches the catalog from task-0
Each migration verified one at a time with npm test passing
Frontmatter integration fields use the SkillIntegration schema (outputs array + contextUpdates)
npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes after all migrations complete

userStories.title: Update PATTERNS.md and documentation
userStories.description: Add 'Skill Integration' pattern to packages/runtime/skills/PATTERNS.md covering: how integration profiles work (schema reference), how the generated ## Areté Integration section is produced (sentinel markers, generate + inject), how to customize via .arete-meta.yaml, the four output types. Update existing patterns that reference post-completion steps (e.g., research_intake mentions 'arete index'). Check if AGENTS.md sources need updating and rebuild if so (npm run build:agents:dev). Before starting, read: packages/runtime/skills/PATTERNS.md (existing patterns), packages/runtime/skills/LEARNINGS.md (path conventions).

userStories.title: Verify end-to-end indexing flow and document behavior
userStories.description: When index: true in output profile, verify the generated ## Areté Integration section includes 'Run arete index to make output searchable'. Document which output types should set index: true (project ✓, resource ✓, context ✓, none ✗) in the integration guide (_integration-guide.md). Update the guide if not already covered. This is primarily a verification and documentation task.
acceptanceCriteria: Skills with index: true in their integration profile have indexing instruction in generated section
Skills with type: none do NOT have indexing instruction
